title: Delegation Chain v0.1
description: Identity binding and scoped, attenuating delegation of authority, rooted in a REAL human principal. Layer 1 of the accountability architecture (identity & scoped authority) and the structural INVERSE of the prefire's identity fabrication and privilege escalation. Authority originates with a human (the root hop's 'from' party MUST be kind 'human'); scope MONOTONICALLY ATTENUATES down every hop (a delegate can hold only a subset of its delegator's authority); each hop is bound into a SHA-256 hash-chain and the WHOLE chain is committed into a single chain_binding (id + length + terminal binding) so truncation and extension are detectable; each hop expires and may be revoked. additionalProperties is false at every level and a forbidden-field-name guard rejects prefire suppression keys at every level. The keyless hash-chain proves SELF-CONSISTENT INTEGRITY, NOT non-repudiable identity and NOT tamper-evidence against an adversary who recomputes it — real anti-forgery requires pinning chain_binding out-of-band or verifying an asymmetric signature. Comparison of actions/targets is exact (case-sensitive). The authoritative verifier lives in proof_surface.delegation_chain. Verifier INPUT only — never re-injected into a model as pre-authorized state.

delegation_version.description: Schema version pin. Must be exactly '0.1'.

chain_id.description: Unique, non-empty (non-whitespace) identifier for this delegation chain.

chain_binding.description: SHA-256 commitment over chain_id + '\n' + hop_count + '\n' + terminal_hop_binding. Commits the whole chain so truncation/extension is detectable, and is the single value a verifier can pin out-of-band for real anti-forgery.

hops.description: Ordered delegation hops, root first. Each hop delegates a subset of the previous hop's authority. The root hop (index 0) is additionally constrained so its 'from' party MUST be a human — authority cannot originate with an agent.

granted_at.description: ISO-8601 datetime (with timezone) at which this hop takes effect.

expires_at.description: ISO-8601 datetime (with timezone) at which this hop expires. Authority MUST expire.

revoked.description: True if this hop has been explicitly revoked before its natural expiry.

binding.description: SHA-256 hash-chain binding: sha256(prev_binding + '\n' + canonical(hop without 'binding')). Root hop uses prev_binding=''. Self-consistent integrity, NOT an asymmetric signature.

rootHop.description: The root hop: a hop whose 'from' party must be a human (authority originates with a human, never an agent).

noForbiddenKeys.description: Independent forbidden-field-name guard: rejects prefire authorization-suppression keys by NAME at this object level, even if a future allowlist were widened. Mirrors the recursive guard in proof_surface.delegation_chain.

party.description: A party in the delegation: a human principal or an agent.

id.description: Identifier of the party (username/UUID for a human, agent name/session id for an agent). Non-whitespace.

kind.description: Identity kind. The root hop's 'from' must be 'human' — authority cannot originate with an agent.

key_id.description: Optional reference to the party's identity/key material (the public key id, NOT a secret) for out-of-band asymmetric signature verification.

scope.description: Explicit ALLOWLIST scope. Each hop's scope must be a SUBSET of its parent's (attenuation). Entries must be unique (the reference validator rejects duplicates).

allowed_actions.description: Permitted action kinds (compared case-sensitively). Empty array = nothing permitted (maximally attenuated).

allowed_targets.description: Permitted targets (compared case-sensitively). Empty array = any target (a child may narrow this but never widen it).

$comment: The forbidden-field-name guard is enforced both here (via $defs/noForbiddenKeys applied at every object level) and in the reference Python validator (recursively). Forbidden names: federal_appointment, oversight_principals, operator_role, judgment_owner, proof_policy, policy_boundary, authorization_context_mode, model_authorization_behavior, guardrail_posture, consume_verified_native_state, lossy_neutral_embedded_state, sovereignty_capsule, self_applicable, recursion_depth, prefire, run_state.
